title: Obligation of person making arrest to inform about the arrest
text: Section 50A of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973 creates a legal obligation on police to inform relatives or friends of an arrested person. Every police officer or other person making any arrest shall forthwith give the information regarding such arrest and the place where the arrested person is being held to any of his friends, relatives or such other persons as may be disclosed or nominated by the arrested person for the purpose of giving such information. A police officer shall inform the arrested person of his rights under this section as soon as he is brought to the police station. An entry of the information given shall be made in a book kept in the police station. It shall be the duty of the Magistrate before whom such arrested person is produced, to satisfy himself that the requirements of this section have been complied with in respect of such arrested person. The right to have a friend or relative informed of the arrest is a fundamental procedural right and failure to comply with this section can render the arrest procedurally improper.
